injuryUpdates.injuryType: Anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) rupture, right knee

lastMatchPlayed.notes: Suffered the ACL injury during this match; underwent successful surgery in Doha

injuryUpdates.source: Daily Beirut, Arab News, Jordan Times - May 7-15, 2026
injuryUpdates.notes: MAJOR BLOW: Officially ruled out of the 2026 tournament by Prince Ali bin Al-Hussein. Scored 8 goals during qualifying and was Jordan's primary striker. Despite extensive rehab he could not recover in time. Club corrected from Young Boys to Al-Arabi Doha, where he has been since August 2024.

lastMatchPlayed.notes: Suffered ACL injury during the final

injuryUpdates.notes: Starting right-back ruled out of the 2026 tournament. JFA confirmed via Prince Ali bin Al-Hussein that rehabilitation efforts were insufficient to restore him in time. Compounds existing right-back depth problem.

injuryUpdates.expectedReturn: Ruled out of the 2026 tournament; long rehabilitation expected

lastMatchPlayed.notes: Ruptured Achilles during solo training; immediately ruled out of the tournament

injuryUpdates.source: Arab News, YSscores, Devdiscourse, Geo Super - May 15, 2026
injuryUpdates.notes: 26-year-old right-back. Third Jordan defender/right-back ruled out (after Al-Naimat and Al-Quraishi). JFA announced he will require a long period of treatment and rehabilitation. Severe depth problem for Sellami at right-back specifically.

lastMatchPlayed.notes: Scored 10th-minute goal in 2-2 draw; completed full Ligue 1 season

injuryUpdates.notes: Captain and primary creative outlet. Confirmed at Rennes (Ligue 1), not Montpellier as previously recorded - transferred Feb 2025. 6G/6A in 25/26 Ligue 1. Avg FotMob rating 7.06. No injury concerns.

tacticalUpdates.oldValue: Al-Tamari's creativity from the number 10 position combined with quick counter-attacks
tacticalUpdates.newValue: Al-Tamari's creativity from the number 10 position; counter-attacks now lack their primary finisher with Yazan Al-Naimat ruled out for the tournament

tacticalUpdates.oldValue: Limited individual quality at the highest level; Very few players in top European leagues; First tournament appearance - no tournament experience; Can struggle to create against organized defenses
tacticalUpdates.newValue: Same baseline weaknesses, now compounded by losing first-choice striker Yazan Al-Naimat (ACL) and both right-backs Adham Al-Quraishi (ACL) and Issam Al-Sumeiri (Achilles) before the tournament

squadChanges.details: Officially withdrawn from the 2026 tournament delegation by the Jordan Football Association due to unresolved ACL injury sustained in December 2025 Arab Cup quarter-final.
Officially withdrawn from the 2026 tournament squad. ACL tear from Arab Cup final has not healed in time.
Officially ruled out of the 2026 tournament after rupturing left Achilles tendon during individual national-team training. JFA announcement May 15.

bettingOddsUpdate.source: Ladbrokes, FOX Sports market aggregation - May 2026
bettingOddsUpdate.notes: Tournament-winner price approx 1000/1 at Ladbrokes (+100000). Odds to win Group J quoted at +5500 (FOX Sports aggregation, May 2026) - heavy underdog behind Argentina (-250), Austria (+400) and Algeria (+700). Specific to-qualify-from-group price was not consistently available across major books.

summary: Jordan has been hit with a third major pre-tournament injury: right-back Issam Al-Sumeiri ruptured his Achilles in solo training on May 14 and was ruled out by the JFA on May 15. The Jordan Football Association also formally confirmed on May 7 that star striker Yazan Al-Naimat (ACL, sustained at the Arab Cup) and right-back Adham Al-Quraishi (ACL, sustained at the Arab Cup) will both miss the tournament after failing to recover. Coach Jamal Sellami remains in charge and the next outing is a May 31 friendly at Switzerland; no matches have been played since 2026-04-16.
